mappings.rationale: Vaughan's VSI is directly focused on the classification, properties, and occurrence of minerals—the primary subject matter of this section's coverage of mineral constituents.
Zalasiewicz covers igneous, sedimentary, and metamorphic rocks, their classification, formation, and distribution—directly mapping to the section's treatment of Earth's constituent rocks.
Provides a broad geological context for understanding minerals, rock types, fossil fuels, and the rock cycle central to this section.
Glazer covers crystal structure and symmetry, directly relevant to the section's treatment of mineral internal structure, crystal classification, and the silicon-oxygen tetrahedron.
Relevant to the chemical composition of minerals and the occurrence of elements in the Earth's core, mantle, and crust.
Ball's coverage of chemical elements connects to the section's discussion of the occurrence and distribution of elements in the Earth and its envelope.
Atkins provides the chemical foundations for understanding mineral composition, crystal chemistry, and the chemical processes forming rocks.
Covers phase equilibria, thermodynamics, and chemical bonding relevant to mineral associations, phase equilibrium in rocks, and metamorphic processes.
Molnar discusses the rock associations formed in different tectonic environments—oceanic regions, mountain ranges, island arcs—as described in this section.
Redfern covers the composition of the Earth's layers and the formation of different rock types, providing context for the section's treatment of rocks and minerals.
Directly relevant to the formation of igneous rocks from magma, including intrusive, extrusive, and pyroclastic rocks discussed in this section.
Lowrie covers the physical properties of Earth materials and the geochemical evidence used to determine rock and mineral composition at depth.
Thomson covers fossilization processes and the organic content of sedimentary rocks, relevant to this section's discussion of organic-rich sedimentary rocks and fossil fuels.
Ellam covers isotopic analysis of geological materials, relevant to understanding the chemical composition and geochemical differentiation of Earth's minerals and rocks.
Lenton discusses the cycling of elements through the geosphere, biosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere, relevant to the section's treatment of elemental occurrence in Earth's envelope.
Hall covers the physical and mechanical properties of materials including crystalline solids, relevant to understanding mineral hardness, cleavage, tenacity, and optical properties.
